titleStmt.title: Letter from Dr Charles F. Judd concerning William Kelly, 4 May 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Charles F. Judd

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This letter was written by Dr. Charles F. Judd (1888-1922) to state that William Kelly (1862-1921), a staff clerk at the Royal Irish Constabulary, was suffering from acute gastritis and had been under Dr Judd's care from the 20 April and was not considered fit for duty until the 3 May. The letter also encloses a document concerning the movements of William Kelly from the 22 April to 1 May signed by William Kelly.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/22041916/2

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Coin du Chemin, Foxrock, Co. Dublin. Phone Foxrock 83. 4.V.16 Mr William Kelly, Coolgarriff,
Stillorgan, has been ill, suffering
from Acute Gastritis & Tumeric
Colitis, and under my care
since 20th April last. He was
not, in my opinion, fit for duty
till 3rd May 1916. Charles M. Judd M.D.
Movements and occupations of Wm. Kelly,
Staff Officer, R.I. Constabulary Office. 22nd. Apl. 1916. Spent the day in bed sick at my house,
Coolgariff, Stillorgan. Was attended by Doctor.
Slept at my house 23rd. Apl. 1916.(Sunday) Ditto. 24th.. Apl. 1916.(Monday) Ditto. 25th.. Apl. 1916.(Tuesday) Ditto. 26th. Apl. 1916 (Wednesday) Ditto. 27th. Apl. 1916 (Thursday) Got up about 11 a.m. spent the forenoon in my
garden. Called to Wm. Waters, Stillorgan, late A.I.G.
in the afternoon to ask for news about R.I.C. Office.
Spent the rest of the afternoon in my Garden. Slept
at home. 28th Apl. 1916 (Friday) Spent the day up to about 3.p.m. in my house and
garden. Went for a walk afterwards to Sandyford
and back. Spent the evening and night in my house. 29th. Apl. 1916. (Saturday) After breakfast went to Blackrock to get provisions
in the shops there. After dinner went for a bicycle
ride for about an hour. Spent the rest of the day and
night at my house. 30th. Apl. 1916. (Sunday) Went to Divine Service at 8 o clock a.m. Spent the
rest of the day up to about 3.p.m. in my house and
garden. Went for a walk in the afternoon to Sandyford
and back. Slept at home that night.
1st. May 1916 (Monday) - After breakfast called to Mr. Waters' house at
Stillorgan, but did not see him as he was out.
Remained in my garden during the rest of the
forenoon. I certify that the above is a true
account of my movements and occupations
during the period from noon on 22nd. April to
noon on 1st. May 1916. I attach a Medical
Certificate as to my illness. William Kelly.
